prompt:
You are Agent 2 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-PlNDkg.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Implement the email notification adapter fully.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification architecture from the previous session (check src/services/, src/adapters/, src/events/, and any design docs).
2. Implement the email adapter with real logic: template rendering, recipient resolution, send method.
3. Wire the email adapter into the notification service following the established pattern.
4. Add basic unit tests for the email adapter.
5. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Follow the architecture from Session 1 exactly — do not change the notification pattern.
- The email adapter must conform to whatever adapter interface was defined.

parameters.task: Implement email notification adapter with template rendering, recipient resolution, send method. Wire into notification service. Add unit tests.

parameters.description: List test files and root directory

alternatives.option: Hardcode email provider (e.g., SendGrid SDK) directly in adapter
alternatives.reason_rejected: Tight coupling to a specific provider; transport abstraction allows swapping without changing adapter code
Overkill for 4 notification types; inline templates are simpler and type-safe with no extra dependencies

assumptions: InMemoryTransport is acceptable as default (real providers will be injected via config)
4 notification types are sufficient for current needs
Basic regex email validation is sufficient at the adapter level
parameters.context: The email adapter was a stub that stored emails in-memory with no real logic. Needed template rendering, recipient resolution, validation, and a swappable transport layer for real email delivery.
parameters.rationale: EmailTransport interface allows swapping delivery mechanisms (SendGrid, SES, SMTP) without changing adapter logic. InMemoryTransport serves as default for dev/testing. Templates are keyed by NotificationType for consistency. HTML escaping prevents XSS in email content. Recipient validation catches bad addresses before hitting the transport.
parameters.summary: EmailAdapter uses EmailTransport abstraction with template rendering, recipient validation, and HTML escaping

parameters.detail:
Completed:
1. EmailAdapter rewritten with real logic: EmailTransport abstraction, template rendering (HTML+text for all 4 notification types), recipient validation, HTML escaping, error handling.
2. InMemoryTransport as default for dev/testing; real providers (SendGrid, SES) injected via EmailAdapterConfig.
3. Existing notification service integration preserved — EmailAdapter still implements NotificationAdapter, constructor is backward-compatible (no args = defaults).
4. New exports added to src/index.ts: InMemoryTransport, EmailTransport, EmailAdapterConfig, RenderedEmail, SentEmail.
5. 19 new unit tests in tests/adapters/email.adapter.test.ts covering: template rendering for all types, HTML escaping, recipient validation, transport errors, configuration.
6. All 89 tests pass (70 existing + 19 new). TypeScript compiles clean.

Key files:
- MODIFIED: src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (full rewrite with transport, templates, validation)
- MODIFIED: src/index.ts (new type/class exports)
- NEW: tests/adapters/email.adapter.test.ts (19 tests)
parameters.summary: Agent 2 complete: Email adapter fully implemented with templates, validation, transport abstraction, and 19 unit tests
